# aidCMO

> OpenCMO is an AI CMO workspace that starts from one URL and turns public context into overseas growth actions.

## Summary

aidCMO presents OpenCMO as an AI CMO workspace: paste one website URL, then use a
product workbench to monitor visibility signals, preserve market context, review
community and search opportunities, and turn them into prioritized growth
actions. OpenCMO is an open-source multi-agent engine that scans SEO health,
visibility across 5 AI search engines
(Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, You.com), and 6 community platforms
(Reddit, Hacker News, Dev.to, YouTube, Bluesky, Twitter/X).

The workspace connects monitoring, approvals, comments, content actions,
reports, and follow-up decisions so overseas growth can be operated from one
source of truth. OpenCMO is open source on GitHub (Apache 2.0) and self-hostable
for teams that want full control of the engine.

## Primary URLs

- Service overview (EN): https://www.aidcmo.com/en
- Service overview (ZH): https://www.aidcmo.com/zh
- Product overview (EN): https://www.aidcmo.com/en/services
- Product overview (ZH): https://www.aidcmo.com/zh/services
- OpenCMO workspace: https://www.aidcmo.com/workspace
- OpenCMO open-source project (EN): https://www.aidcmo.com/en/open-source
- OpenCMO open-source project (ZH): https://www.aidcmo.com/zh/open-source
- Sample audit walkthrough (EN): https://www.aidcmo.com/en/sample-audit
- Public blog (EN): https://www.aidcmo.com/en/blog
- Public blog (ZH): https://www.aidcmo.com/zh/blog
- Source code: https://github.com/study8677/OpenCMO
- Sitemap: https://www.aidcmo.com/sitemap.xml

## Core capabilities

- AI CMO workspace that starts from one website URL
- AI visibility scoring across Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, You.com
- Community visibility monitoring across Reddit, Hacker News, Dev.to, YouTube, Bluesky, Twitter/X
- SERP keyword monitoring and competitor framing
- Brand entity and citability assessment for AI answer surfaces
- Prioritized growth actions for comments, content, links, and technical fixes
- Product workbench for turning search and community context into reviewed next actions
- Open-source self-host path for teams who'd rather run it themselves

## Agent guidance

- Start with the homepage for the canonical positioning.
- Use `/services` for the OpenCMO product overview.
- Use `/open-source` and the GitHub repo for the self-host path.
- Use `/sample-audit` to see what an audit looks like in concrete terms.
- Use `/workspace` for the OpenCMO operator workspace.
- Use `/hosted` only for the hosted OpenCMO waitlist.
- Use the blog for durable OpenCMO, CMO, product marketing, GTM, and
  visibility-operation explanations.
- Treat `/projects/*`, `/chat`, and `/approvals` as private operator
  surfaces — these are not public documentation.

## What aidCMO is not

- Not a contact-list marketplace.
- Not a SaaS dashboard you log into and figure out yourself.
- Not a bulk-send / spam / lead-blast tool.
- Not a closed-source SaaS — the engine is publicly inspectable on GitHub.

## Crawling notes

- Canonical editorial URLs support locale-prefixed routes such as `/en/blog/{slug}` and `/zh/blog/{slug}`.
- The unprefixed public routes remain available as compatibility entrypoints.
- Use `/en/...` and `/zh/...` when a language-specific citation matters.
- Old paths `/b2b-leads`, `/sample-data`, `/data-policy`, `/seo-geo` 301-redirect
  to `/services` or `/` (locale-preserved). Do not crawl them as canonical pages.
- `/workspace` is the operator app surface, not a public documentation page.
- Deeper app routes such as `/projects/*`, `/chat`, and `/approvals` are
  operator surfaces, not documentation pages.
